name: WriteByteArraySignalAsInteger
title: WriteByteArraySignalAsInteger
syntax: dword WriteByteArraySignalAsInteger(signal aSignal, qword value, dword byteOrder, dword byteCutMode) // form 1
dword WriteByteArraySignalAsInteger(pdu aPDU, char signalName[], qword value, dword byteOrder, dword byteCutMode) // form2
description: Writes the value of a byte array signal, treating it as a 64-bit integer. Use this function if you previously wrote the value of a byte array signal with the .raw64 selector. This selector works only for integer signals, but byte array signals were treated incorrectly as integer signals in CANoe versions < 12.0 SP3.

parameters.name: aPDU
parameters.description: An AUTOSAR PDU with a byte array signal

parameters.name: signalName
parameters.description: Name of the signal on the PDU

parameters.name: byteOrder
parameters.description: Byte order for interpreting the signal bytes. 0: little-endian (intel) 1: big-endian (motorola) See examples below.

parameters.name: byteCutMode
parameters.description: How the additional bits of the 64-bit value shall be cut if the signal has less than 64 bits. 0: ignore bits a the end 1: ignore bits at the beginning See examples below.

description: If a signal has 56 bits (7 bytes) and the value shall be written in motorola (big-endian) byte order and the given value is 0x9123456789ABCDEF, depending on the byteCutMode parameter, the following bytes will be set in the signal:
If the value shall be written in intel (little-endian) byte order, depending on the byteCutMode parameter, the following bytes will be set in the signal:
example.code:
byteCutMode == 0: 91 23 45 67 89 AB CD
byteCutMode == 1: 23 45 67 89 AB CD EF
byteCutMode == 0: EF CD AB 89 67 45 23
byteCutMode == 1: CD AB 89 67 45 23 91

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
